Despite life-saving efforts, the infant tragically died just before midnight. A medical examiner later determined the child's cause of death to be quote, complications of environmental heat exposure and deemed the manner of death an accident. Prosecutors later weighed pressing criminal charges against the couple, but ultimately decided not to, over quote, insufficient evidence to support conviction at trial due to issues involving the cause of death. This decision came after an autopsy report concluded the baby had choked on regurgitated milk, and what the family's lawyer described as a freak accident.
